claim: vibe-halt builds and its full gate battery passes on the audited tree: make test and make gate both exit 0.
subject: vibe-halt
scope: working tree 3e2a5ed (pinned public 84f911e), macOS arm64, python3.11 shim, 2026-07-21

evidence.note: cargo test --workspace, exit 0
full gate battery, exit 0

justification: Both commands were executed and transcripts recorded with exit codes.
falsifier: A rerun of make gate on the same tree exiting nonzero.

claim: make onboard fails on stock macOS because check_determinism_denylist.py requires tomllib (Python >= 3.11) and system python3 is 3.9.6, with no interpreter version check.
subject: vibe-halt-onboard

evidence.note: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'tomllib', verdict NOT READY, exit 2
same command with python3.11 shim: READY, exit 0

justification: Failure and py3.11 success were both reproduced and recorded.
counterevidence: CI on ubuntu-24.04/py3.12 is unaffected, so the failure is invisible to CI
falsifier: A stock macOS host where make onboard exits 0 at this commit.

claim: Cross-process determinism holds: the same demo-buggy command run twice as separate processes produces byte-identical output.
subject: vibe-halt-determinism

evidence.note: diff of two full outputs: identical (diff exit 0; both runs exit 1)

justification: Two independent process invocations were diffed byte-for-byte.
counterevidence: Single platform only; cross-OS bit-identity rests on the CI verify matrix, not re-executed here
falsifier: Any byte difference between two same-command runs, on any platform.

claim: Replay of a failing universe reproduces the exact finding (same violated keys and hash), and replay of a non-failing universe returns UNCHECKED (exit 3) as documented.

universe 0 replay: exit 3, replay verdict UNCHECKED

falsifier: A replay whose violated keys or hash differ from the batch run.

claim: A hypervisor/process-level determinism layer (Antithesis Determinator, Hermit, rr) is correctly out of scope for vibe-halt's next phase; the mission is served at the process/protocol boundary.

counterevidence: dhyve shows an OSS deterministic hypervisor is buildable; if Tier-2 subprocess determinism fails its leak battery, this rejection must be revisited
falsifier: R6 leak battery proving process-boundary determinism unachievable at acceptable fidelity.

claim: The initial Track-2 opt-in swarm palette did not beat v0 on the pinned seeded corpus bakeoff; it passed 0/5 classes against the R2 threshold.
subject: swarm-palette-r2
scope: branch claude/track2-w1-swarm-palette, 16 seeds from 0xD1CE, max budget 100, 2026-07-22

evidence.note: python3 scripts/track2_swarm_bakeoff.py --seeds 16 --max-budget 100; OVERALL class_passes=0/5 required=4 enforced=False

justification: The harness ran both palettes over all five pinned classes and 16 seeds, recording first detection and budget-to-target; no class met the configured 0.25 speedup threshold.
counterevidence: The algorithm shipped as opt-in only; a different swarm-mask algorithm or larger budget could perform differently, so this falsifies only this implementation and threshold run.
falsifier: A rerun of the same harness on the same code with class_passes >=4/5.

claim: Event-priority (PCT-inspired) scheduling d=3 over same-timestamp choice points showed no advantage over uniform-with-random-tiebreak at finding VB-006 (pct_wins=0/32, losses=8) — a NARROW null: the first-failing-universe metric was saturated (floor effect), so this does not generalize to guided exploration. Combined with VH-TRACK2-NULL-001 (swarm 0/5, discriminating instrument), guided exploration remains unproven on this rig and investment stops.
subject: pct-scheduler-c2
scope: branch claude/convergence-c2-pct, 32 seeds from 0xD1CE, budget 1000, VB-006, 2026-07-22

evidence.note: python3 scripts/track2_pct_bakeoff.py --seeds 32 --budget 1000; median_pct=0 median_uniform=0 pct_wins=0 losses=8 ties=24; FIFO v0 0/10000 red-check; PCT d=3 76/100 first=0

justification: VB-006 exposes 6 independent two-way races per universe; uniform tiebreak finds it with per-universe probability 1-(1/2)^6 ~= 98.4% (observed 96/100), so both medians saturate at 0 and the metric cannot discriminate strategies (Codex audit C.1, issue #24). Within that limit, PCT-inspired scheduling won zero head-to-heads and lost eight.
counterevidence: The instrument, not the algorithm class, bounds this claim: a depth->=2 bug class with low uniform hit probability could still separate event-priority scheduling from uniform. Claim narrowed 2026-07-22 from 'falsification-complete' per Codex audit C.1 — the broad wording over-reached the metric's discriminating power.
falsifier: A seeded depth->=2 same-timestamp bug class whose uniform per-universe hit probability is low (<~10%), where event-priority scheduling's median first-finding beats uniform over 32 seeds on this harness.
